title: Stomatanthes dyctiophyllus
taxonomy: Stomatanthes dyctiophyllus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant belongs to the genus Stomatanthes, which is part of the Heliantheae tribe. Further classification within Stomatanthes dyctiophyllus includes the family Asteraceae and the order Asterales. The plant's classification is further broken down to the kingdom Plantae.
constituents: Stomatanthes dyctiophyllus is known to contain various active constituents. These include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. Studies have identified the presence of kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, among other flavonoids, in the plant. The specific combination and proportions of these constituents may vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growth conditions. Further research is needed to fully elucidate the composition and activities of Stomatanthes dyctiophyllus' active constituents.
